business.headline: Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets crash again amid rising US-Iran tension?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,995.5 at around 7:50 am, indicating that the NSE Nifty50 could open above Wednesday's closing level of 23,882.05.

US states may sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros merger next week: ReportSeveral US states are preparing to challenge Paramount's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery in court. The move could delay the deal, deepen financial pressure and intensify antitrust scrutiny.

US court approves Musk-regulator settlement over Twitter shares despite 'red flags'A US judge approved the SEC's settlement with Elon Musk over delayed disclosure of Twitter share purchases. The order also raised questions about why the regulator eased its claims and settled through Musk's trust.

New EPFO portal: Here's how it could make PF claims easier and faster for employeesWith the new centralised system, member services can now be processed by any EPFO office across the country. This is expected to improve efficiency, reduce waiting time and ensure quicker resolution of requests.

IMF cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.4%, but expects strong momentum to continueIn its July 2026 World Economic Outlook (WEO) Update, the IMF projected India's economy to grow by 6.4% in FY2026-27, slightly lower than the 6.5% forecast made in April.

Not Mumbai, not Delhi: This city has India's highest household incomeIf you thought Mumbai or Delhi had India's highest-earning households, think again. A new income ranking has placed another city at the top, with both metros trailing behind.

ITR-5 filing begins: Check eligibility, deadlines and common errors before you fileThe wait is over for taxpayers who file ITR-5. With the Excel Utility now available, here's everything you need to know before filing your return.

Dalal Street bleeds as soaring crude and geopolitical worries spook investorsThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,677.12 points, or 2.15%, to close at 76,503.60, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 516.65 points, or 2.12%, to settle at 23,882.05.

Dalal Street bloodbath: Sensex tanks 1,600 points, more pain ahead for investors?As of 2:42 pm, the sell-off on Dalal Street intensified, with the benchmark BSE Sensex plunging 1,766.68 points, or 2.26%, to 76,414.04, while the NSE Nifty50 tumbled 553.35 points, or 2.27%, to 23,845.35 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp spike in crude oil prices above $78 per barrel and weak global cues triggered broad-based selling across sectors.
RBI backs crypto ban, tax department cites tracking and tax risksIndia has not yet taken a clear stand on cryptocurrencies, with policy discussions continuing for several years.

New EPFO portal is here. What does it mean for your PF account?EPFO has given its digital platform a major upgrade, bringing all key PF services under one roof. But how will this benefit crores of EPF members? Let's have a look.

US-Iran tensions are back: So, is gold going up?Whenever tensions flare up in the Middle East, gold usually grabs investors' attention. But with the US-Iran conflict back in focus, is the yellow metal finally gearing up for a rally?

South Korea enters bear market as AI chip rally falters despite Samsung profitSouth Korea's KOSPI slipped into bear market territory as investors sold AI-linked chip stocks despite Samsung's record profit forecast. The swing reflected growing doubts over whether the AI-led chip boom can sustain current valuations.

ITR filing alert: 7 tax rules every intraday and F&O trader should knowTax filing has become more detailed for intraday and F&O traders this year. Knowing a few key rules before filing your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices and compliance hassles.

The hidden tricks making you spend more online: Why Sebi may finally step inA study found dark patterns on most listed consumer transaction platforms and urged Sebi to seek self-declarations. The report casts deceptive design as both a consumer protection issue and a corporate governance risk.

Name one car damaged by ethanol fuel: Nitin Gadkari dares E20 criticsUnion Minister Nitin Gadkari defended the E20 petrol rollout and challenged critics to name any damaged car. His remarks came as concerns over mileage, older vehicles and a possible move beyond E20 intensified.

Crude oil rally hits Dalal Street: IndiGo, HPCL, BPCL fall up to 5%A rise in crude oil prices after fresh US-Iran tensions dragged aviation and oil marketing stocks lower on Dalal Street. InterGlobe Aviation, the parent company of IndiGo, dropped more than 3%, while SpiceJet also traded lower.

Sensex ends 1,677 points lower: 3 reasons why stock market crashed todayAs of 1:56 pm, selling pressure intensified on Dalal Street, with the benchmark BSE Sensex tumbling 1,122.46 points, or 1.44%, to 77,058.26, while the NSE Nifty50 plunged 342.60 points, or 1.40%, to 24,056.10 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp rise in crude oil prices and weak global cues triggered broad-based risk-off sentiment across equities.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as US-Iran tensions push crude above $76The BSE Sensex was down 535.44 points, or 0.68%, at 77,645.28, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 158.45 points, or 0.65%, to 24,241.00 at around 9:25 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market crash today?Sensex and Nifty are headed for a weak start after the US-Iran flare-up shook global markets and lifted crude prices. GIFT Nifty was trading at 24,246 around 8 am, down 164 points.

entertainment.headline: Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj to be screened at gurudwaras across five states Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj is set to reach audiences through community screenings at gurudwaras across five states after being removed from ZEE5 in India. The initiative aims to keep the story of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra alive despite the film's OTT takedown.

Alpha box office Day 6: Alia Bhatt's spy thriller nears Rs 75 crore globally Actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha continued its steady box office run on Day 6, collecting Rs 2.85 crore nett in India. The YRF Spy Universe film is now just short of the Rs 75 crore worldwide milestone.

If Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj unsettled you, watch top 7 films on police brutality Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj revisits Jaswant Singh Khalra's probe into illegal cremations and custodial killings in Punjab. A seven-film watchlist places it alongside Indian titles examining police violence, caste and institutional power.

Rob Reiner earns posthumous Emmy nomination for The Bear role Actor and filmmaker Rob Reiner has earned a posthumous Emmy nomination for his guest role in The Bear, marking his first acting nod since 1978. The legendary filmmaker is nominated in the Guest Actor in a Comedy Series category.

Satluj actor Suvinder on people holding community screenings: They are doing seva Actor Suvinder Vicky said people across Punjab are organising screenings of Satluj after its removal from ZEE5. He said the public response has outweighed the controversy and deeply moved the film's team.

Entertainment News Today Live: Gurudwaras to screen Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj amid row Welcome to the world of entertainment. We bring you all the latest happenings from films, OTT, television and celebrity buzz throughout the day. Our live coverage features breaking news, exclusive interviews, movie announcements, box office updates and much more.

Emmy Awards 2026 nominations full list: The Bear, Beef and Hacks in spotlight The 2026 Primetime Emmy Awards nominations were announced on Wednesday by Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller. The list puts major comedy, drama and anthology contenders including The Bear, Hacks, The Pitt, Slow Horses and Beef in the spotlight.

Alliance: Sohail Khan makes emotional confession after Seema Sajdeh's entry Actor Sohail Khan got emotional after his ex-wife, designer Seema Sajdeh, entered Alliance and took responsibility for their split. Before entering the show, Seema said they would always remain allies on the show.

Dhamaal 4: Jaaved Jafferi says first film was marketed wrong, became a cult later As Dhamaal 4 heads for release, actor Jaaved Jafferi said the first film was marketed wrongly despite its uniqueness. In an exclusive interview, he said the franchise later found its audience.

Sanjay Gupta slams IMDb as bogus after Satluj's 9.5 rating vanishes Days after Satluj was taken down from ZEE5, its 9.5 IMDb user rating disappeared from the platform. Sanjay Gupta called IMDb "bogus", while Niren Bhatt said there had been no communication around the developments.

Gauahar Khan calls Messi actor of saddest order after Argentina vs Egypt match Actor Gauahar Khan criticised footballer Lionel Messi after Argentina's 3-2 win over Egypt. Her post added to the wider row over the refereeing controversy in the Argentina-Egypt World Cup match.

Gaurav Khanna to enter Lock Upp, confront wife Akanksha over divorce revelation Actor Gaurav Khanna will enter Lock Upp after his wife Akanksha Chamola revealed their separation and plans to divorce. The episode will be the first time both speak about their strained marriage on national television.

Sourav Ganguly approves Dada poster as internet debates Rajkummar Rao's casting Former Indian cricket captain Sourav Ganguly praised the first look of Rajkummar Rao's Dada and said it revived memories of the 2002 NatWest final. The poster drew mixed reactions online as the internet debated the actor's casting.

The world feels quieter: Maniesh Paul bids emotional farewell to mother Urmil Actor Maniesh Paul paid an emotional tribute to his mother, Urmil Paul, after her death on Wednesday. His heartfelt Instagram post reflected on their close bond and the emptiness her loss has left behind.

Ramayana trailer to be launched this month: Check date, time and venue Director Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana trailer is set to be unveiled this month. The launch opens the film's full-scale campaign for the two-part epic ahead of its Diwali 2026 release.

Inside Anshula Kapoor's reception: Ranveer-Arjun light up stage, Alia-Orry pose Anshula Kapoor and Rohan Thakkar ended their wedding celebrations with a grand Mumbai reception attended by family, friends and film personalities. Videos of Ranveer Singh-Arjun Kapoor's dance, Himesh Reshammiya's live set and pictures shared by Orry have gone viral.

Saif Ali Khan's beach look has wife Kareena Kapoor saying what everyone's thinking Kareena Kapoor Khan shared fresh beach holiday pictures of Saif Ali Khan and called him her hot husband. The post drew fan praise for Saif's beach look and revived memories of his Race 2 appearance.

Actor Rajesh Sharma hospitalised after suspected insect bite on Prabhas film set Actor Rajesh Sharma suffered a suspected insect bite while shooting for a Prabhas film at Ramoji Film City. He is under close observation in a Kolkata hospital as doctors monitor a fast-spreading leg infection and possible complications.

Exclusive: Ikka director decodes Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna's acting process on set Director Sidharth P Malhotra, in an exclusive interview with India Today, opened up about directing Sunny Deol and Akshaye Khanna in Ikka. He said their contrasting methods, along with his nightly edit screenings, built trust on set.

general news.headline: How Pak's own missiles shoot down its sham bid to claim pre-Islamic rootsPakistan is making a sham bid to claim the pre-Islamic cultural and civilisational heritage of the Indian subcontinent. However, a concerning amount of Pakistan's military hardware, especially its missiles, are named after Turkic and Afghan conquerors who plundered the region for centuries.

US launches fresh strikes on Iran hours after Trump declared peace deal overThe United States launched fresh strikes on Iran, with CENTCOM saying the operation aims to weaken Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz after recent attacks on commercial vessels.

Telangana techie charged with wife's murder in US, sent photo of body to lover in IndiaA Telangana-born software engineer strangled his wife just six months after their marriage. Police allege the killing was motivated by his ongoing secret relationship with another woman in India.

Spain back on Trump's good books, he says NATO ally very generous. Here's whyUS President Donald Trump said Spain agreed to additional payments after a bitter NATO summit clash over defence spending and Iran. Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez called their exchange cordial as US agencies examined possible trade steps against Spanish goods.

Mohamed Salah denied clear penalty vs Argentina? New angle proves referee rightEgypt feel that they were denied a clear win against Argentina in the Round of 16 in the FIFA World Cup 2026. One of the major inflection points occurred when Mohamed Salah was denied a penalty in stoppage time, a decision that could have changed the result of the game.

Jaipur woman hires contract killers to murder her own mother for government jobPolice claimed that Ayushi Sharma plotted her mother Neeraj Sharma's murder with the help of relatives and contract attackers, allegedly driven by the lure of family property and a government job.

Pune manager rejects leave despite heavy rain, employee asks if he should quitA Pune employee told his manager he could not come to work because of heavy rain and waterlogging, but the leave request was rejected.

Trump launches first sweeping probe into H-1B visa fraud, official names CognizantUS Labor Department Inspector General Anthony D'Esposito said investigators had already issued dozens of summons as part of what he called the administration's most aggressive effort yet to tackle foreign labour fraud.

Video: Raigad floods wash away 3,000 LPG cylinders; public warned not to touchHeavy rain flooded the HPCL Patalganga LPG Bottling Plant, sweeping around 3,000 cylinders into the river. Authorities have warned residents not to touch any cylinders found downstream.

F-35 fighter jets for Turkey? What Trump's move could mean for India, IsraelDonald Trump's decision to lift sanctions on Turkey and reopen the door to F-35 sales marks a dramatic reversal of US policy. The move could reshape the strategic balance in the Middle East, unsettle Israel's military edge, and raise fresh questions for India amid Turkey's growing defence partnership with Pakistan.

Bengaluru's second airport to come up in south of city, announces DK ShivakumarKarnataka has moved ahead with plans for Bengaluru's second airport in the city's southern corridor. The site will be chosen to limit costs, ensure feasibility and avoid displacing villages.

Trump ditches new Qatari Air Force One amid Iran tensions, flies back in older jetTrump returned from the NATO summit in Turkey aboard the older Air Force One instead of the newly retrofitted Qatari-gifted jet, making a surprise aircraft switch as the US and Iran resumed exchanging strikes.

Japan is making electricity from salty water and sewage. The science is realJapan has switched on its first osmotic power plant in Fukuoka, generating electricity by mixing desalination brine with treated sewage water. Here is the science of blue energy, explained in the simplest terms.

Smoothie tasted like cough syrup: Clue in wife's murder by Telangana techie in USOn the day she was killed by her husband, Raajitha Sabbineni messaged him that a smoothie he had prepared tasted like 'cough syrup', raising suspicions that he may have attempted to poison her before the murder.

No Trump card: FIFA rejects France appeal over star player's yellow before quartersWorld Cup: FIFA rejected France's appeal against Michael Olise's yellow card before the Morocco quarter-final. The ruling leaves their star winger one booking away from a semi-final suspension.

Live: Heavy rain in Delhi-NCR, commuters brave severe waterloggingMonsoon Tracker Live Updates: Heavy monsoon rains battered large parts of India on Wednesday, triggering flash floods, waterlogging and major disruptions to rail and road traffic, while rescue teams raced to reach people trapped in rain-related incidents across several states. In Maharashtra's Pimpri Chinchwad, 11 people were feared trapped after a garbage mound collapsed following incessant rainfall. Rescue operations were underway. In Gujarat, Surat witnessed flood-like conditions after receiving 358 mm of rain in the 24 hours ending Wednesday morning. At least nine people have died in rain-related incidents in the district over the past few days. Heavy rains also triggered a debris collapse in Kerala's Wayanad and flash floods in Jammu and Kashmir's Doda district, while several other parts of Maharashtra reported rain-related deaths and widespread disruptions. Union Home Minister Amit Shah spoke with the chief ministers of Kerala, Maharashtra, Gujarat and Jammu and Kashmir to review the situation and assured them of all possible assistance from the Centre. The downpour also drenched the national capital, leaving several areas waterlogged and slowing traffic across the city. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued red and orange alerts for Delhi, warning of more thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and heavy rain in several districts. Mumbai also continued to reel under waterlogging as persistent rainfall affected normal life in parts of the city. In Uttarakhand, the IMD issued an orange alert warning of heavy rain accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning in Dehradun, Uttarkashi, Haridwar, Tehri, Rudraprayag, Pauri, Chamoli, Almora and Bageshwar districts. Stay with Indiatoday.in for latest updates…

Amaravati vs MaViGun: How Andhra capital remains a political fault lineAmaravati vs MaViGun: Twelve years after bifurcation, Andhra Pradesh's capital debate is far from settled. With Amaravati now the legal capital and Jagan pitching MaViGun as an alternative vision, the battle over the state's future is set to define Andhra Pradesh's politics ahead of 2029 polls.

The initiative aims to keep the story of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra alive despite the film's OTT takedown.

Will get worse if...: Trump warns Iran after fresh US strikes over ship attacksSharing visuals of fresh US strikes on Iranian sites, US President Donald Trump warned Tehran that if it continued attacking commercial shipping in Hormuz, "it will get much worse."

Emmy Awards 2026 nominations full list: The Bear, Beef and Hacks in spotlightThe 2026 Primetime Emmy Awards nominations were announced on Wednesday by Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller. The list puts major comedy, drama and anthology contenders including The Bear, Hacks, The Pitt, Slow Horses and Beef in the spotlight.

4th T20I preview: How do Shreyas Iyer's India come back from 76-all-outEngland vs India, 4th T20I preview: India have lost the opportunity to win the 5-match T20I series. With 2 matches to go, can Shreyas Iyer's team find it in themselves to dig them out of the hole?

Live: Guard of Honour for PM Modi in MelbournePM Modi in Melbourne Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to hold a series of high-level engagements in Melbourne on Thursday as part of the second leg of his three-nation tour. He began the day by attending the India-Australia CEO Forum and Economic Roadmap Business Reception, which will be followed by engagements at Government House Victoria, where he will co-chair the 3rd India-Australia Annual Summit with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and address the media. Later in the day, PM Modi will interact with the Indian diaspora at a community event. The visit is aimed at deepening the India-Australia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, with discussions expected to focus on defence, trade, education, critical minerals, mobility and people-to-people ties. Stay with Indiatoday.in for latest updates…

Monsoon to take full control over Delhi: Heavy rain to kill humid heatDelhi-NCR is set for a widespread monsoon spell with frequent rain and thunderstorms through Thursday. The active system is expected to pull down temperatures, ease the rain deficit and raise the risk of waterlogging.

Indian oil tanker turns back after Iran rejects Omani corridor in Strait of HormuzThe temporary corridor was announced on June 24 by Oman and the United Nations' International Maritime Organization (IMO) to facilitate the movement and evacuation of vessels stranded in the Gulf amid heightened regional tensions.

sports.headline: No Trump card: FIFA rejects France appeal over star player's yellow before quarters World Cup: FIFA rejected France's appeal against Michael Olise's yellow card before the Morocco quarter-final. The ruling leaves their star winger one booking away from a semi-final suspension.

Flawless Alexander Zverev cracks Fritz code to reach maiden semi-finals Alexander Zverev ended a seven-match losing streak against Taylor Fritz with a commanding straight-sets victory to reach his maiden Wimbledon semi-final. The French Open champion will now face British wildcard Arthur Fery for a place in the final.

FIFA World Cup: Why fans can't keep match balls? Catching a FIFA World Cup match ball may feel like the ultimate souvenir, but fans are expected to return it almost immediately. Here's why FIFA insists on getting every ball back, and how cutting-edge technology inside the official match ball plays a major role.

They were not robbed: Bhaichung Bhutia sides with referee Egypt vs Argentina Former India captain Bhaichung Bhutia believes that the referee took the right calls in Egypt vs Argentina match. Bhaichung says that VAR is not perfect and they are getting questioned for the right reasons.

Who is Arthur Fery? Tennis player whose father owned a football club Arthur Fery's fairytale Wimbledon run continued as the British wildcard stunned ninth seed Flavio Cobolli to reach the semi-finals. This is the biggest victory of the player's career. But who is this new British wonder kid?

Sourav Ganguly inducted into ICC Hall of Fame on 54th birthday Former India captain Sourav Ganguly was inducted into the ICC Hall of Fame on his 54th birthday, becoming the 10th Indian men's cricketer to receive the prestigious honour.

Marta Kostyuk savours Centre Court debut, seals maiden semi berth Marta Kostyuk marked her Centre Court debut with a commanding 6-3, 6-2 win over Jasmine Paolini to reach her maiden Wimbledon semi-final, extending her remarkable run this year.

Mohamed Salah denied clear penalty vs Argentina? New angle proves referee right Egypt feel that they were denied a clear win against Argentina in the Round of 16 in the FIFA World Cup 2026. One of the major inflection points occurred when Mohamed Salah was denied a penalty in stoppage time, a decision that could have changed the result of the game.

Austrian and British GP Review: 2 weekends, 3 races, and 5 key talking points Formula One's Austrian and British Grands Prix delivered drama on and off the track, from Mercedes' growing reliability concerns and Ferrari's title hopes to Max Verstappen's uncertain future, Kimi Antonelli's emergence as a fan favourite, and fresh questions over the FIA's handling of race procedures.

England great Tammy Beaumont to retire from international cricket after Lord's Test England opener Tammy Beaumont will retire from international cricket after the one-off Test against India at Lord's Cricket Ground, ending a decorated 17-year career.

India to miss Pratika Rawal for historic Lord's Test, Priya Punia named replacementIndia have suffered a major setback ahead of the historic women's Test at Lord's, with opener Pratika Rawal ruled out and Priya Punia named as her replacement after an impressive India A tour of England.

Spit, spat and an X: Why Egypt coach Hossam Hassan is being celebrated and dissedEgypt's football head coach Hossam Hassan is at the centre of a raging debate following his team's defeat against Argentina in the FIFA World Cup. From waving the Palestinian flag and flashing FIFA's anti-racism X sign to allegedly spitting at fans waving an Israeli flag, his actions have earned him both praise and derision.

Quarterfinals Highlights: Fery stuns Cobolli, Zverev reaches maiden semi-finalsWimbledon 2026 quarterfinals Highlights: Alexander Zverev and Arthur Fery reached their maiden Wimbledon semi-finals after beating Flavio Cobolli and Taylor Fritz respectively. Earlier, Linda Noskova and Marta Kostyuk advanced to the semis after beating Elise Mertens and Jasmine Paolini.

Furious Egypt demand expulsion of French referee after controversial exitEgypt has demanded FIFA expel French referee Francois Letexier from the FIFA World Cup, alleging discrimination and deliberate VAR errors during their controversial 3-2 Round of 16 defeat by Argentina in Atlanta.

Arnav Paparkar continues dream run, marches into boys' singles quartersIndian junior Arnav Paparkar stormed into the Wimbledon boys' singles quarterfinals with a commanding straight-sets win over Ryo Tabata. The 18-year-old has now won three matches in a row, including a stunning upset of third seed Keaton Hance.

What Messi has that Ronaldo doesn't. No, it's not a World CupLionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo have defined an era, but the biggest difference between them isn't goals or trophies. It's the teams around them. While Argentina built a system that elevated Messi, Portugal are still searching for the right way to maximise Ronaldo and it might be a bit too late.

MAGA playbook: Iran President on Trump's intervention in US's FIFA red card rowIranian President Masoud Pezeshkian attacked the US over the FIFA World Cup red card controversy and likened its conduct to Donald Trump's MAGA playbook. His remarks came minutes after Trump said Washington's interim peace agreement with Tehran was over.

Afghanistan mourn Shapoor Zadran, postpone domestic T20 league as mark of respectThe Afghanistan Cricket Board postponed two days of the Shpageeza Cricket League as a mark of respect following the death of former pacer Shapoor Zadran at the age of 38. The Afghanistan cricket fraternity will gather in Kabul to bid farewell to one of the pioneers of the country's rise in world cricket.

Drop Tilak. Bring back Sanju Samson? Ex-coach warns India after 3rd T20I surrenderAbhishek Nayar has advised India not to reshuffle the XI merely to recall Sanju Samson after the third T20I loss in Nottingham. He said Samson should return only in the top three as India reflect on form and conditions.

Archer fires subtle dig at IPL over easy pitches: Back to normal in EnglandEngland pacer Jofra Archer believes bowling in English conditions is a return to "normal" after spending the IPL on "easy wickets and smaller boundaries" in India, saying bowlers have a much bigger margin for error back home.

Nice to play 90 mins: Djokovic's cheeky Messi quip is viral after Wimbledon marathonAfter a historic five-hour Wimbledon marathon, Novak Djokovic delivered a cheeky nod to fellow 39-year-old icon Lionel Messi, joking that he envies football's strict 90-minute limit after enduring his own open-ended epic.

Is FIFA favouring Argentina? Egypt coach's rigged World Cup charge divides opinionFIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan accused officials of rigging his side's dramatic 2-3 defeat to Argentina after the defending champions staged a stunning late comeback in Atlanta. His allegations drew contrasting reactions from Thierry Henry, Roy Keane and Zlatan Ibrahimovic, reigniting the debate over VAR consistency and refereeing standards.

I'll never watch World Cup again: Egypt coach says FIFA favoured MessiEgypt head coach Hossam Hassan launched an extraordinary attack on FIFA after his side's dramatic FIFA World Cup Round of 16 defeat to Argentina, alleging that the governing body wanted Lionel Messi and the defending champions to remain in the tournament.

Egypt coach booked after anti-racism gesture in World Cup loss to ArgentinaEgypt head coach Hossam Hassan found himself at the centre of controversy after making a gesture towards the match officials during his side's FIFA World Cup Round of 16 loss to Argentina.

Two 39-year-olds, Messi and Djokovic, prove it's never over till it's overAn epic Wimbledon quarter-final. An unforgettable World Cup comeback. On the same Tuesday, Novak Djokovic and Lionel Messi, both 39, produced performances that defied age, expectation and the temptation to believe the story was already over.

Wimbledon: Djokovic likens marathon quarters win to iconic 2019 final vs FedererNovak Djokovic said his record-breaking Wimbledon quarter-final victory over Felix Auger-Aliassime ranks among the finest matches of his career, comparing the five-hour, 15-minute epic to his unforgettable 2019 final against Roger Federer.

Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach first quarter-final since 1954Switzerland reached their first World Cup quarter-final since 1954 after edging Colombia 4-3 on penalties following a goalless draw. Gregor Kobel starred before Ruben Vargas converted the decisive kick to set up a last-eight clash with Argentina.

It was atrocious: Shreyas Iyer shuns cliches after India slip to new lowShreyas Iyer did not hide behind the usual cricketing cliches after India's humiliating 125-run defeat to England in the third T20I. Calling the performance "atrocious", the India captain delivered one of the most brutally honest post-match assessments in recent memory.

Wimbledon: Djokovic survives five-hour epic to set up semifinal clash vs SinnerNovak Djokovic survived the longest Wimbledon quarter-final in history, defeating Felix Auger-Aliassime in a five-hour, 15-minute thriller to reach a record eighth straight semi-final, where he will face defending champion Jannik Sinner.

Tears, triumph and a tribute: Lionel Messi's unforgettable night in AtlantaLionel Messi was overcome with emotion after Argentina's stunning comeback win over Egypt in the World Cup Round of 16. From tears at the final whistle to teammates lifting him in celebration, the unforgettable scenes captured the spirit of the defending champions.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
